translation: And Solomon begot Rehoboam; and Rehoboam begot Abijah; and Abijah begot Asa,

alternate_translations.translation: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam became the father of Abijah, and Abijah became the father of Asa,
alternate_translations.notes: Here “begot” is rendered with the more modern “became the father of,” which keeps the genealogical meaning while using plainer English. It matches the standard sense of ἐγέννησεν (“begot/fathered”).

notes: This is a typical biblical genealogical formula using ἐγέννησεν (“begot,” i.e., “became the father of”). The names are rendered in commonly used English biblical forms: Ῥοβοάμ = Rehoboam, Ἀβιά = Abijah (often also spelled “Abia”), and Ἀσάφ = Asa (note: depending on manuscript/tradition, some editions may read Ἀσά in later Latin/English, but the English “Asa” is the standard identification).
